doc_content.summary: The stark-metrics skill acts as a data pipeline and interactive analysis tool. It ingests run history from local JSON files, processes them via a robust Python backend (metrics.py), and orchestrates an interactive bash layer that prompts users to resolve identified issues and highlights system-wide trends.
key_features: Python-based data aggregation with graceful degradation on corrupted history files
Dynamic output routing between terminal-native formatting and structured JSON
Interactive bash wrapper driving an actionable recommendation engine
Meta-observation heuristics for identifying 'wins' (e.g., lower FP rates) and 'urgent' regressions
Comprehensive custom observability telemetry including filter metrics and record counts
usage_examples: Extending `metrics.py` to ingest new JSON payload structures from future stark skills
Adding new conditional logic to the shell wrapper to prompt specific follow-up skills based on new recommendation types

alt_text: Architectural flow diagram and internal components of the stark-metrics skill, showing data ingestion from history files, Python script processing, interactive output handling, and observability telemetry.
